children.name: Cariama
children.description: A monotypic genus of long-legged, terrestrial bird with a distinctive crest of upright feathers at the base of the bill. Seriemas are among the last surviving members of the Cariamiformes, an ancient bird order that also included the giant, flightless phorusrhacids or 'terror birds'. They are excellent runners and can fly short distances when necessary.

children.name: Cariama cristata
children.description: A large, striking bird of the grasslands and cerrado of Brazil, Bolivia, Uruguay, Paraguay, and northern Argentina. It has a grey body, a red bill and legs, and a distinctive crest of erectile feathers. Its loud, far-carrying yelping call is often delivered as a duet and can be heard from several kilometres away.

children.name: Chunga
children.description: A monotypic genus of seriema similar to Cariama but slightly smaller and less crested. The Black-legged Seriema occupies drier, more arid habitats than its red-legged relative.

children.name: Chunga burmeisteri
children.description: Found in the dry Chaco and inter-Andean valleys of Bolivia, Paraguay, and Argentina, this species prefers arid scrub and dry forest. It is slightly smaller and greyer than the Red-legged Seriema, with black legs and a less prominent crest. It feeds on snakes, lizards, insects, and seeds, often stunning larger prey by bashing it against the ground.
